name: Nevada Theater
self_description: The oldest, continuously operated theatre venue on the west coast of the United States. Over 155 years of history, live performance, music, film, and theatre in Nevada County, CA.
notes: Clear business identity with founding date and mission

name: Sierra Stages
self_description: Sierra Theatre Company is proud to bring affordable, accessible, and high-quality theatre to Nevada County, CA. With a passionate and talented team at the helm, we are excited to carry on the legacy of delivering top-notch performances.
notes: Clear first-person mission statement from About page

name: Stardust Station
self_description: Stardust Station is an art collective, including onsite artist studios and performance venue.
notes: Direct self-description from About page

name: A Serious Production
self_description: A Serious Production is an audio production house that provides podcast production service, DJ services, sound engineering, & mobile stage production. Founded by CharRon Smith in 2018.
notes: Clear mission statement with founder and founding year

name: Ben Franklin
self_description: We have been a family owned and operated business in Grass Valley, since 1973. We strive to bring you the best customer service, along with the most wanted products.
notes: First-person mission statement with founding year

name: Country Copy
self_description: Country Copy has 35 years of commercial printing experience and ranks 167 out of the top 400 Printers in the US. Our ability to print and manage any job in-house separates us from our competition.
notes: Clear business identity with experience and competitive advantage

name: Gold Crush
self_description: Gold Crush Climbing Gym was forged in 2017 by a close-knit community of climbers. Custom design and hand built by the owners and community, Gold Crush is one of the most unique gyms in Northern California.
notes: Clear founding narrative with community involvement

name: Heart & Soul Spinnery
self_description: Heart & Soul Alpacas and Guest Cabins consists of Russell Ratti & Karen Ball. We are a family owned and operated business and Alpacas are all we do.
notes: Clear first-person business description with founders

name: House of Print and Copy
self_description: House of Print and Copy specializes in full service custom printing with remarkably fast turn around times at competitive prices. We are a local company that understands what it takes to serve our market.
notes: Clear mission statement with service focus

name: Louvre Gallery
self_description: Locally owned and operated, we have been serving the Grass Valley, Nevada City communities and beyond for over 45 years and two generations. We are an art gallery with local artists, art restoration and framing shop.
notes: Clear business identity with multi-generational history

name: Mountain Recreation
self_description: Welcome to Mountain Recreation, the Sierra Foothill's premiere source for outdoor gear, apparel, seasonal ski and kayak rentals. Locally owned and operated since 2000.
notes: Clear welcoming mission with founding year and market positioning

name: Myrtle Tree Arts
self_description: At Myrtle Tree Arts, we believe that art has the power to transform lives and bring communities together. Our events are designed to nurture creativity and inspire artistic expression.
notes: Clear first-person mission statement

name: Riparian Studios
self_description: Kit Kohler founded Riparian Studios in Nevada City after nearly a decade making ads for national brand campaigns in San Francisco. The move was deliberate: toward clients whose work matters to this specific place, and away from distance-produced content that never quite lands.

name: SRC Event Rentals
self_description: Since 1975, SRC Event Rentals has been at the forefront of making every event in Nevada County unforgettable. Our legacy is built on providing unparalleled customer service and an extensive selection of party and event rental equipment.

name: Sierra Custom Ink
self_description: For over 40 years, our family-run team has been weaving together expertise across the creative industries, marketing, design, and—more recently—screen printing, embroidery, and promotional branding. Having lived, worked, and rooted ourselves here in Nevada County, this is more than just business—it's home.

name: Someone Said Yes DJ and Photo Booth
self_description: A Boutique DJ & Photo Booth Company. We have two home bases. One in St. Augustine, FL, and a second in Nevada City, CA. Music is just part of the job—we're here to bring the energy, manage the vibe, and create the kind of dance floor memories that live in group chats forever.

name: Waking Crow Studio
self_description: Waking Crow is the working name of a multimodal artist, poet, and photographer working and living in the ancient Nisenan Lands that border the Tahoe National Forest.

name: Yinne Boma Animation Studios Company
self_description: Our mission is to Entertain the world through Animation, Cartoon Films & TV Presentations, Live Action Films & TV Shows and Other Media, Entertainment and Arts. Our Motto: Imagine It, Believe It, Live It, Do It.

name: Yinnebowma
self_description: Our mission is to Entertain the world through Animation, Cartoon Films & TV Presentations, Live Action Films & TV Shows and Other Media, Entertainment and Arts. Our Motto: Imagine It, Believe It, Live It, Do It.
notes: Duplicate of yinne-boma-animation-studios-company (same website, likely duplicate entry).

name: You Bet! Bicycle Sales & Service
self_description: Gateway To The Sierra. Fast, friendly, and professional bicycle repair. We service all bikes and love everything on two wheels! Focused on engaging and expanding the mountain biking, bikepacking, road, and gravel community.

name: Brown Banana Books
self_description: The funny, tropical-themed bookstore
notes: Tagline present; limited descriptive text beyond the bookstore concept.

name: Clock Tower Records
self_description: Classic Vintage Vinyl, New Vinyl, CDs, Audio Components, Music Related Gift Items, Special Orders. Buy, Sell, Trade.
notes: Service description rather than self-narrative; no founder or history story.

name: Fieldmouse Press
self_description: Fieldmouse Press is a nonprofit publisher of comics and literature, based in Grass Valley, California. Founded in 2019 to publish SOLRAD, the online literary magazine for comics, we are committed to expanding the reach and appreciation of the comics arts.

name: Foggy Mountain Music
self_description: Nestled in Grass Valley, California since 1975, our store stands as a treasure trove for all things musical. We pride ourselves on offering a unique blend of musical instruments, which goes beyond the offerings of conventional music retailers.

name: Gold City Guitar Repair
self_description: As a builder of instruments, as well as a player, I am keenly aware of the dynamic relationship one may have with an instrument. My intent with Gold City Guitar is to provide a personalized and intimate service to the Sierra foothills community.

name: KVMR 89.5FM
self_description: Music of the world. Voice of the community.
notes: Tagline visible but limited narrative. About/mission page not fully captured in pages data.

name: Nevada County Doris Foley Library for Historical Research
self_description: The Doris Foley Library for Historical Research is a non-circulating branch of the Nevada County Library system. It is housed in a Romanesque Revival architectural style Carnegie library building completed in 1907.
notes: Descriptive but factual/architectural focus rather than mission narrative.

name: Nevada County Library Grass Valley Royce Branch
self_description: The Nevada County Library system consists of six library locations. In 1902, a free library and reading room was established in Nevada City. The Vision is a center of enrichment, in a strong, empowered, and literate rural community.
notes: Institutional, mission-focused text but not direct first-person voice.

name: Nevada County Library Madelyn Helling Branch
self_description: The Nevada County Library system consists of six library locations. In 1902, a free library and reading room was established in Nevada City. The Vision is a center of enrichment, in a strong, empowered, and literate rural community.
notes: Institutional text. Same pages as first venue (shared website).

name: San Juan Ridge Community Library
self_description: The mission of the San Juan Ridge Community Library is to provide an inspirational and educational hub while giving the residents, including students, of the San Juan Ridge excellent library services including book exchange, DVD rentals, and internet access in a central location.

name: Sibylline Press
self_description: Sibylline Press publishes the work of brilliant women over 50, exclusively. We are looking for work that is relevant and compelling. Sibylline's publishing engine is powered by the ingenuity of shrewd wise women. Sibylline Press takes its name from the Sibylline Books, the ancient writings of wise older women, the Sibyls.

name: Cirino's at Main Street
self_description: Our story began in 1983 when Jerry Cirino first opened the doors to Cirino's Bar & Grill, an Italian Restaurant in Nevada City, offering 'time honored' Italian recipes from the Cirino family kitchen.

name: Corvus Bakery
self_description: As the only GF bakery in Nevada County, we are dedicated to offering you a creative twist on classic bakery favorites, in a facility that is 100% Gluten-Free.

name: Crazy Horse Saloon & Grill
self_description: Our identity is synonymous with world-class live music, elevated bar food using organic ingredients, a full range of libations, sporting spirit and an overriding comfort factor that makes us a home away from home for many.

name: Deer Creek Bakehouse
self_description: Deer Creek Bakehouse, lead by Graeme Aycock, launched in 2021 from a tiny house on Deer Creek in Nevada City with dreams of sharing delicious, hand-crafted bread with the people.

name: Diego's Restaurant
self_description: Diego's celebrates hand-prepared latin inspired recipes from Chile up to California for over two decades. By operating with a culture of love, gratitude and a professional personal touch, we enjoy providing a colorful, eclectic and comfortable atmosphere.

name: Friar Tucks Restaurant & Bar
self_description: Inside a landmark 1860 brick building, Friar Tuck's has been Nevada City's most beloved table since 1973, offering live music every night and a Wine Spectator award-winning cellar.

name: Gary's Place
self_description: Gary's Place is a lively cocktail bar in Grass Valley, CA, offering a full kitchen with fresh food, karaoke nights, and a pool table with a Main Street patio for outdoor seating.
notes: Text is from Nextdoor business listing, but captures venue's self-presentation

name: Gold Miners Inn
self_description: Gold Miners Inn Grass Valley an Ascend Collection Hotel is a 2.5-star property located in Grass Valley, California, steps from Grass Valley Brewing and serving as a convenient base for exploring the region's mining heritage and cultural attractions.
notes: Description from third-party booking site, not direct venue content

name: Golden Gate Saloon
self_description: The Golden Gate is one of the longest operating barrooms west of the Mississippi River, serving classic and seasonal cocktails, regional and international wines, and a selection of beers with live music nights and local character.

name: Grass Valley Brewing Co.
self_description: Grass Valley Brewing Company came to life because of our passion for great beer and love of community. We believe great beer is produced when skill meets ingredients.

name: Grass Valley RV Resort
self_description: Tucked into the rolling hills of California's Sierra Nevada foothills, Grass Valley RV Resort is where the spirit of Gold Country meets modern comfort. We're not just a stop along the road — we're a destination where families, friends, and fellow travelers gather to celebrate life, adventure, and community.

name: Harmony Ridge Lodge
self_description: Harmony Ridge Lodge, nestled in the woods of the Tahoe National Forest 5 miles from Nevada City, is one of Nevada County's true gems. We specialize in Weddings, Retreats and Bed & Breakfast.

name: Holbrooke Hotel
self_description: We've been building character since 1852. Celebrating Grass Valley at its most authentic—unearthed, restored and brought forward into today. After serving the community for over 150 years, the Holbrooke Hotel is ready to spend some time with you.

name: Kaido
self_description: Kazuhiko Mukai, Sushi Chef and owner of the Kaido Japanese Restaurant in Grass Valley, grew up in restaurant family in Japan. Kaido offers both sushi and tempura.

name: Lazy Dog Chocolateria
self_description: Our mission is to connect Grass Valley with a sweet sense of place, a hometown atmosphere and put a smile on everyones who walks through our door. We use all the original recipes for your favorite treats.

name: Lola at the National Exchange Hotel
self_description: Cuisine at Lola is high-spirited and modern, sparked by the great wild west and a shine of European finesse; a dedication to beautiful ingredients, locally sourced, sustainable, and seasonally inspired.

name: Lucchesi Tasting Room
self_description: Founded in 2000, Lucchesi Vineyards was born from a shared dream of Mario and Linda Clough, to craft exceptional wines from their scenic Grass Valley estate, nurtured by family and community.

name: Maria's Mexican Restaurant
self_description: Family restaurant serving homecooked authentic Mexican food for over 30 years. Located in Grass Valley, CA Maria's serves authentic Mexican food created from scratch.

name: Marshall's Pasties
self_description: At Marshall's Pasties, we pride ourselves on crafting delicious, hand-held meals that celebrate tradition and flavor. Nestled in Grass Valley, CA, our story begins with a passion for quality ingredients and authentic recipes.

name: MeZe Eatery
self_description: Mezé, a Middle Eastern Eatery offering conscious, healthy, organic and simple food, a casual and warm atmosphere, ordering convenience, excellent customer service and attention to detail.

name: Mountain Ranch Winery
self_description: The secret that makes Mountain Ranch Winery special stems from winemaker Bob Hoffman's passion for winemaking. Mountain Ranch Winery was established in 2003.

name: Naggiar Vineyards and Winery
self_description: Naggiar is much more than just an off-the-beaten-path treasure, our winery is the source of some of California's most desirable grapes. Pair unprecedented wines with our picturesque one hundred thirty-five acre estate.

name: National Exchange Hotel
self_description: Built in 1856 in what was to become the most sophisticated of the gold rush mining camps, the National Exchange Hotel is a treasured piece of Nevada City history.

name: New West Wine Co.
self_description: New West Wine has moved to our new location at 126 Mill Street in Grass Valley. We're open for bottle sales as well serving wines beers by the glass.
notes: Limited self-description; mostly operational details

name: Panadería Matilija
self_description: Panadería Matilija is located in picturesque Nevada City, California. Housed in the town's oldest kitchen in a historic building dating back to the 1850s, the panadería serves up handmade Mexican pastries.

name: Pete's Pizza
self_description: Family Owned And Operated! We specialize in crafting thin-crust pizzas made fresh daily with homemade dough and our secret recipe sauce, promising an authentic flavor in every bite.

name: Restaurant DaKan
self_description: Restaurant DaKan was founded with a passion for sustainable, community-focused dining. We source our ingredients from local farms and transform them into inspired dishes with global influences.

name: Sierra Starr Tasting Room
self_description: The Starr family has been producing award winning wines in the Sierra Foothills since 1996. We take pride in crafting small lot wines from our estate vineyards, specializing in Zinfandel, Sauvignon Blanc and Cabernet Franc.

name: Sushi at Nourish
self_description: Hello friends, we're Lacie and Ryan Whittington. Born and raised in Nevada County, we're dedicated to the health and well-being of our community. That's why we created Nourish Sushi.

name: Taqueria El Taco Loco
self_description: Taqueria El Taco Loco features authentic and traditional Mexican dishes, including our famous tacos, burritos, enchiladas, sizzling fajitas & more!

name: Taste of Thai
self_description: We are honored to serve you! We would like to take this opportunity to welcome you to your home to great combination of Thai dishes. We serve only the freshest and finest ingredients.

name: The Owl Tavern
self_description: We are two couples with a dream of opening a restaurant in our hometown of Grass Valley, CA. Backed with culinary and agricultural backgrounds, we pride ourselves by providing humanely raised meat and seasonally sourced produce.

name: The Parsonage 1865
self_description: Experience the beauty and charm of this small-town gem, situated in the foothills of the Sierra Nevada Mountains. The Parsonage 1865 is a 10 Bedroom/10 Bathroom Victorian Estate with the capacity to accommodate up to 23 guests.

name: The Willo Steakhouse
self_description: Established 1969. A Nevada County institution. Voted best steak year after year by the readers of The Union. Over 1 million hand cut, choice, aged New York and Ribeye steaks cooked on our grill.

name: Three Forks Bakery & Brewing Co.
self_description: Since 2014 Three Forks Bakery & Brewing Co has specialized in organic, farm fresh fare. We do many things including a microbrewery, full service coffeeshop, bakery, and farm to table restaurant.

name: Tofanelli's Gold Country Bistro
self_description: Tofanelli's Gold Country Bistro is renowned for many things: Award-winning patio dining, amazing breakfasts, 101 omelet menu, an incredible burger menu. Established in 1890.

name: Wild Eye Pub
self_description: A vibrant, friendly, multi-use venue, anchored by a large dining room with a seasonal, organic and local menu, featuring nightly live music and events, and a full-service bar.

name: Art Space
self_description: Art Space is not only a place for making art but also a therapeutic (art) SPACE that holds room for emotional processing and personal growth through creative expression.

name: Bear River Glassworks
self_description: Glass Flameworking Teaching Studio & Creation Center. We offer classes and workshops for all skill levels with bench space rental, tools, and raw materials available on site.

name: Eric Rood Administration Center Lobby
self_description: Art in Public Spaces is a program of Nevada County Arts & Culture in partnership with the County of Nevada. Public art must always strive to be physically and freely accessible.

name: Firehouse No. 1 Museum
self_description: The historic Firehouse No. 1 museum is a diamond located in the heart of historic downtown Nevada City. Completed in the Spring of 1861, this firehouse was home to Nevada Hose Company No.1 from 1861 to 1938.

name: Nevada County Narrow Gauge Railroad Museum
self_description: Located in Nevada City, California, the Nevada County Narrow Gauge Railroad Museum is dedicated to the preservation of local transportation history and artifacts from the narrow gauge railroad era.

name: North Star Mining Museum
self_description: The North Star Powerhouse Mining Museum, a part of the Nevada County Historical Society, exists to preserve and protect our area's mining heritage and educates people about the mining process.

name: The Chambers Project
self_description: The Chambers Project stands as a beacon of psychedelic art and culture, representing some of the most influential contemporary artists in the field. Founded and curated by Brian Chambers, a passionate advocate for the psychedelic experience, the gallery has become a global hub for artists, enthusiasts, and collectors alike.

name: UBA SEO: Nisenan Arts and Culture
self_description: 'Uba Seo: Nisenan Arts and Culture is a gallery and culture center that brings visibility to the Nevada City Rancheria Nisenan Tribe in their Ancestral Homelands.

name: Cosmic Shark Clothing
self_description: Shop our original designs all hand printed by us!
notes: Tagline about their work; not a full first-person self-description but conveys core identity.

name: Earthdrop
self_description: Earthdrop Designs provides design and fabrication services, as well as fast turnaround CNC and laser cutting/engraving services to businesses, creative agencies, and individuals.
notes: Descriptive about services but not first-person narrative (uses 'provides' rather than 'we provide'). Note: website text indicates Los Angeles location, but may be outdated.

name: El Barrio Mexican Market
self_description: El Barrio is more than just a Mexican market—it's a community gathering place that celebrates Mexican culture and heritage. We are locally owned and operated in the Grass Valley community dedicated to giving back.

name: Elevation 2477'
self_description: At Elevation, we help people define, discover, and achieve a deeper sense of personal well-being through cannabis. We believe in taking a modern approach to holistic wellness, while empowering people to make informed choices about what brings them relief and joy.

name: Empire Shoes
self_description: We are a quality shoe store offering the best brands, custom shoe-fitting, and personalized attention. Empire Shoes is Nevada County's locally owned premium shoe store with a wide selection of the very best in comfortable shoes for work or play.

name: Foothill Flowers
self_description: When you shop flowers online with Foothill Flowers, you will see beautiful bouquet arrangements of flowers hand-crafted with passion, attention to detail, and great care. Foothill Flowers is committed to creating beautiful flower arrangements and floral gifts for any occasion.

name: Fur Traders
self_description: In 1978, Fur Traders was established in Nevada City, California, by an enterprising couple who brought traditional fur garments, pelts & hides back to our historic Gold Rush Era community. Fur Traders has since delighted generations of locals & visitors alike, with a flair for the unique, the glamorous, and the oft outrageous.

name: GoodTimes Boardstore
self_description: Since 1997, Goodtimes has been holding it down as the exclusive core skate shop in the surrounding area. We pride ourselves in being the backbone of the skate community, and have no plans of deterring from that path.

name: J. Breuer Clothing
self_description: We're a clothing store for adventurous men and women, or the adventurous at heart. We stock name brands such as Simms, Pendleton, Lazy One, Newari, and so much more!

name: Moms & Minis
self_description: Moms & Minis was founded in 2018. We are a locally owned boutique featuring a carefully curated collection of responsible clothing and accessories for expecting moms and their minis.

name: Nightingale Farms
self_description: My name is Lateefah and I am the owner/operator/maker at Nightingale Farms. We are dedicated to providing the highest quality animal care that creates quality natural products.

name: Stucki Jewelers Inc.
self_description: We uphold our legacy of superior service, by enhancing relationships through the emotion of fine jewelry.

name: Sugar and Spice
self_description: Sugar And Spice is dedicated to providing you with the best lingerie shopping experience possible. We believe that every woman deserves to feel confident and beautiful in her own skin, and that starts with the right foundation.

name: Tess' Kitchen and Culinary
self_description: Our independently owned and operated flagship store is a hidden gem in the heart of Historic Downtown Grass Valley, CA. With 8,000 square feet of pure culinary paradise, we have everything you need to bring your kitchen dreams to life.

name: The Hat Store
self_description: The Hat Store offers a wide variety of stylish men's, women's, and children's hats from formal, casual, recreation, gardening, sun-protection, weddings, baseball caps, western, fedoras, and genuine handwoven Panama hats.

name: The Melo Plant Shop
self_description: I'm Melody and welcome to my slice of nature, The Melo Plant Shop, where we cultivate a passion for plants and bring nature's beauty into your home.

name: The Phoenix Rose
self_description: We are realizing that in essence we are warriors, and we are regal...we are rugged, and we can express this rugged beauty through how we present ourselves to the world.

name: Tour of Nevada City Bicycle Shop
self_description: We're passionate about bicycles. We believe that bikes offer a simple solution to many of our communities' complex problems.

name: 107 Argall Way
self_description: Onyx Theatre Premier Independent Film House. 2 theatres|Seating 29 each in plush velvet chairs. A bespoke destination for the 21+ audience. Beer|wine|local eats.

name: Behind the Iron Gate
self_description: We invite you to experience the ultimate in family fun or company team building at Behind the Iron Gate in Grass Valley. Our escape rooms are physical adventure games where players enter one of our themed environments and must use elements of the room to solve a series of puzzles, riddles, and challenges to progress and ultimately "escape."

name: LeGacy Presents
self_description: Filling the Stage with stories, dance and musical performances, LeGacy Presents' purpose is to Entertain. We adore our audience and the community in which we live.

name: Paul Emery Presents
self_description: The "Nevada City Live" series marks Emery's 41st anniversary of producing concerts and gatherings that create unforgettable moments for both the performers and attendees.

name: Brothers Arts & Antiques
self_description: John is a CAGA certified antique appraiser, art and antique collector, and has been restoring art and antiques for forty five years. The Wibberley Gallery represents 45 years of creating, collecting, and restoring all genres of art.

name: Enid and Edgar Vintage
self_description: unique vintage clothing - quality construction - specializing in high quality materials - vintage and newer books of high accord - always fair pricing

name: Grass Valley Antique Emporium
self_description: The Antique Emporium specializes in high-value collectibles and rare artifacts, catering to discerning collectors and connoisseurs.

name: Lost and Found Vintage
self_description: We specialize in western style & rare finds!

name: Solstice
self_description: Quality Resale Clothing for both men and women, Vintage, Costumes, Jewelry & Accessories.

name: Sunchild's Parlour
self_description: Sunchild's Parlour BLOOMED ON the Legendary Haight street in San Francisco, California and has NOW officially BEEN TRANSPLANTED to the SierRa mountains.

name: Ananda Village: Crystal Hermitage Gardens
self_description: A place of peace, meditation, and beauty.

name: Cascade Canal Trail:Banner Mountain
self_description: This popular trail offers an almost level walk along a peaceful canal through a forest with many Douglas firs and dogwoods.
notes: Managed by Bear Yuba Land Trust; descriptive trail information

name: Condon Park Trails
self_description: Condon Park was donated to the City in 1957 by Lulu Condon.
notes: City of Grass Valley park information page

name: Empire Mine: Memorial Park Trail
self_description: The trail from Memorial Park brings you right into the heart of Empire Mine State Historic Park where you can tour the visitor center, picnic, hike further on an extensive web of trails and see historic buildings and mining artifacts.
notes: Managed by Bear Yuba Land Trust

name: Empire Mine: Osborne-Hill Trails
self_description: The Osborne Hill Trail area makes up the southern arm of Empire Mine State Historic Park.
notes: Managed by Bear Yuba Land Trust

name: Empire Mine: Union Hill Trail Area
self_description: The Union Hill Trail Area is part of the 800-acre Empire Mine State Historic Park.
notes: Managed by Bear Yuba Land Trust

name: Hoyt Trail - South Yuba River
self_description: This trail leads to swimming holes along the South Yuba River and historic Hoyt Crossing.
notes: Managed by South Yuba River State Park

name: Kenny Ranch Trails
self_description: Wildflower Ridge Trail winds through Wildflower Ridge Preserve just west of Grass Valley.

name: Loma Rica Trail
self_description: This commuter trail runs alongside Brunswick Road; after it crosses Idaho-Maryland Road, it ascends through meadows and woods to Loma Rica Road.
notes: Managed by Bear Yuba Land Trust

name: Malakoff Diggins State Historic Park
self_description: Malakoff Diggins State Historic Park is nestled amongst the pine-studded chaparral forest of the Sierra Nevada Foothills and is home to California's largest hydraulic gold mine.
notes: California State Parks official page

name: Narrow Gauge Trail
self_description: This is a well-used local trail running through the countryside north of Chicago Park.
notes: Managed by Bear Yuba Land Trust

name: Nevada City Airport Trails
self_description: Old lightly used trail on city property overseen by Bear Yuba Land Trust.
notes: Trailforks trail aggregator; managed by Bear Yuba Land Trust

name: Rattlesnake Trail
self_description: Located off Rattlesnake Road in Grass Valley, this lovely, quiet forested walk with an irrigation canal and mining history was a joint project of the Rattlesnake Neighborhood Association and Bear Yuba Land Trust.
notes: descriptive text from trail listing; Bear Yuba Land Trust website rather than venue's own site

name: Spaulding Reservoir
self_description: The lake was originally built for hydraulic mining in 1912, but today campers and boaters enjoy this beautiful lake.
notes: descriptive text from informational page; not first-person from venue operator

name: Sugarloaf Mountain
self_description: Early in 2011, the City of Nevada City acquired Sugarloaf Mountain, the iconic landmark just north of town; it is now a 30-acre open space preserve.
notes: trail facts/description; not direct self-description from venue

name: The Independence Trail
self_description: Founded by the late John Olmsted, the Independence Trail transformed a historic gold mining ditch into the nation's first identified handicapped-accessible wilderness trail.
notes: clear statement of founding and mission

name: Walking Tour of Nevada City Trees
self_description: The self-guided walking (or driving) tour of native and non-native trees in the downtown Nevada City area was originally prepared by horticulturist and tree care consultant Ellen Solomon in 2008, along with contributors Randall Frizzell, Roger McGehee and Chet Blackburn.
notes: clear origin story and mission statement

name: Yewei'Im Bom Trail
self_description: This one-mile exterior loop trail was completed in the spring of 2016. It traverses a variety of landscapes such as meadow, chaparral, mixed conifer forest and wetland habitat.
notes: trail facts; not direct first-person self-description

name: InnerRhythms Dance Theater
self_description: For two decades InnerRhythms has served the Truckee-Tahoe community through the Performing Arts. It is our mission to use the power of dance and music to cultivate life skills in our youth, promote global stewardship and create an everlasting passion for the arts.
notes: first-person mission statement from About page

name: Trails & Vistas
self_description: Trails & Vistas mission is to create community by celebrating the arts and nature.
notes: clear mission statement

name: Truckee Tahoe Theatre Company
self_description: Truckee Tahoe Theatre Company's mission is to provide community members of all ages with an inclusive space to engage in high-quality performing arts, inspire creativity, and bring people together to experience the transformative magic of live theater.
notes: clear mission statement from About page

name: Filmprojects
self_description: We are a non-profit dedicated to promoting the advancement of LGBTQ+ people, other marginalized groups, and the film community as an independent film company. We strive to make films and celebrate cinematic works that are culturally diverse, socially conscious and spiritually positive.
notes: first-person 'We are' mission statement

name: Granite Chief Ski & Mountain Shop
self_description: For over 40 years, Granite Chief has been a trusted name in Truckee's ski community known for expert boot fitting, race-ready tunes, and deep roots in the Sierra. BlueZone Sports has now joined forces with Granite Chief and will operate as Granite Chief Powered by BlueZone Sports in our Truckee location.
notes: clear origin and current business description

name: Gratitudes Gifts & Home Decor
self_description: Gratitudes Gifts & Home Decor began as a Gift Basket Venture in 1996, owned and operated by Sue Moule and Julie Huck out of Sue's family home. Due to the high demand for their offerings, the mother-daughter team quickly diversified and opened its beautifully eclectic storefront in 2000 and Gratitudes has continued to thrive since its inception.
notes: detailed founding story

name: Love and Lupines Floral Design
self_description: Love and Lupines is a floral design studio based in Truckee, CA. We have been creating stunning floral arrangements for weddings and other special events since 2015.
notes: clear first-person origin and mission

name: Mountain Forge Inc.
self_description: We are a family owned and operated blacksmith shop in Lake Tahoe. We handcraft custom metal work.
notes: first-person description of business model

name: Snowy Mountain Tattoo Co.
self_description: Founded in 2018 to preserve and celebrate tattoo art, history, and culture, Snowy Mountain Tattoo brings together artists from across diverse cultures and backgrounds.

name: Swazzle Inc.
self_description: Swazzle Inc. is a California-based puppet company that offers a wide range of services, including custom-designed and-built puppets, rental puppets, live puppet shows, and puppet production services.
notes: clear service description

name: TIP Printing & Graphics
self_description: Our goal is to achieve your vision because your success is also our success. We are experienced in digital color imaging, multi color offset printing, letterpress & mailing services.
notes: first-person mission and service description

name: Tahoe Mountain Sports
self_description: At TMS, we are deeply committed to our community, the environment, education, and our valued customers. We are a locally owned and operated business nestled in Truckee, California, a small town filled with great adventures.
notes: first-person values and identity statement

name: Tahoe Print Shop
self_description: Tahoe Print Shop owner/operator Grant Barta has more than 25 years in the printing and publication industry. As a photographer, writer, publisher, graphic designer, print production manager, sign maker, and print quality control technician Grant brings a wide gamut of experience to Tahoe Print Shop.
notes: founder background and expertise

name: Tahoe Sports Hub
self_description: We are a family owned and operated outdoor sporting goods retailer operating in a 130 year old historic building in downtown Truckee California. We are committed to offering the largest selection of inventory and the best customer service in the Truckee-Tahoe region.
notes: first-person description of ownership and commitment

name: Technical Equipment Cleaners
self_description: Technical Equipment Cleaners is North Lake Tahoe's first and most experienced outdoor gear cleaning and repair company. We've restored, repaired, and re-waterproofed your adventures since 2006!

name: The Office Boss
self_description: The Office BOSS - Getting your To-Do list done!

name: Totally Board
self_description: Truckee's Premier Snowboard & Skate Shop Since 1994

name: Truckee Boardhouse
self_description: Truckee Boardhouse is first and foremost a family operation, started by a husband and wife team that met working in a snowboard shop. We wanted to create a shop that felt welcoming and catered to the whole family and all levels.

name: W&T Graphix
self_description: W & T Graphix has produced tens of thousands of personalized T-shirts hats and other promotional items each year. The business has been located in Truckee since 1992 when owners Wes and Tonya Beyer moved it from its original location in Reno, Nevada.

name: Moonshine Ink
self_description: Moonshine Ink is North Lake Tahoe/Truckee's independent newspaper, on streets since 2002. We produce a monthly print publication with 24,000 readers along with thousands more a month on this website.

name: Nevada County Library Penn Valley Branch
self_description: The Nevada County Library system consists of six library locations.

name: Sierra Sun
self_description: The Sierra Sun, formerly known as the Truckee Sun, and its predecessors have been published in Truckee since 1869. One of California's longest-running local newspapers, rooted in Truckee since 1869.

name: Split Rock Music Co.
self_description: Located in the heart of Downtown Truckee, Split Rock Music is your musical destination in the North Lake Tahoe region. We proudly offer a diverse array of services, including music lessons and a comprehensive retail selection of instruments and music supplies.

name: Truckee Tahoe Radio - 101.5 KTKE
self_description: Our Mission is to entertain and inform the local community. We are INDEPENDENT so we can play and talk about what YOU want to hear. Our format is Triple A, which means we play a wide variety of music.

name: Word after Word Books
self_description: Word After Word Books is a local Indie Bookstore located in Historic Downtown Truckee. We strive to be a welcoming space for our community and visitors to connect around books.

name: Alibi Ale Works
self_description: We opened the doors to our humble brewery in North Lake Tahoe back in 2014 with a commitment to world-class beer, community-building, and good times for all! Today our public houses in Incline Village, NV and Truckee, CA are welcoming community hubs with a laid-back mountain vibe where happy people gather for fresh beer, tasty food, live music, and special events.

name: Alpenswing
self_description: Alpenswing offers a lively and welcoming atmosphere perfect for all ages, combining great drinks, tasty light snacks, and fun for the whole family. Guests can enjoy our state-of-the-art golf simulators featuring Trackman technology for a realistic and engaging experience, as well as thrilling axe throwing.

name: Blue Cow Deli
self_description: A small-town sandwich shop in a friendly & scenic country setting. Blue Cow Deli offers the area's finest lunch menu. With a spacious ranch-style dining room and a lovely dog-friendly patio the deli offers a unique and comfortable experience.

name: Blue Coyote Bar & Grill
self_description: Founded with a passion for adventure, authenticity, and community, Blue Coyote Truckee is more than just a brand—it's a lifestyle rooted in the wild, expansive spirit of Northern California and the endless possibility of the outdoors.

name: Casa Baeza Mexican Restaurant
self_description: Indulge in the authentic flavors of Mexico at Casa Baeza in Truckee, California, where culinary tradition meets taste in every dish. With over 30 years of local legacy and an impressive 500+ 5-star Google reviews, Casa Baeza has become a beloved icon in the community.

name: Cloud Sushi
self_description: Cloud Sushi started in June 2022 when Owner/Head Chef Josh Brown learned of a vacancy in the space being rented by Tahoe Sports Hub for storage. The idea was to have a commissary kitchen space for his private chef business, but realized there was a service window for pickup orders.

name: Como
self_description: COMO was created as a collaboration of local artists, designers, and makers from Tahoe.

name: Cottonwood Restaurant & Bar
self_description: At Cottonwood, we believe great meals should feel like home - warm, generous, and worth slowing down for. That's why we cook with care, source with intention, and treat hospitality like a craft.

name: Dark Horse Coffee Roasters Truckee
self_description: We practice appreciation for our evolution. We share in hopes to guide, connect, and inspire. The secret is in the senses, the magic is in you.

name: Donner Lake Inn
self_description: We are your hosts, Nina and Jim Meehan and family! We are delighted to welcome you to the Donner Lake Inn. We live on property in the main house.

name: Drunken Monkey Sushi
self_description: We love sushi, and we love Truckee, so it was natural for us to combine the two. At Drunken Monkey, we constantly innovate to bring new dishes to life.

name: El Toro Bravo
self_description: Located in Historic Old Town Truckee, El Toro Bravo has been serving authentic Mexican cuisine and cocktails since 1988. El Toro Bravo both in Capitola (50 years) and Truckee (28 years) were started by Delia "Grandma" Rey.

name: FiftyFifty Brewing Co.
self_description: Craft means doing it all. We source everything ourselves; from our hops, to our water to our cans. Every decision is a balance between the best possible experience, and the best possible business.

name: Full Belly Deli
self_description: Full Belly Deli was born over a fresh-baked loaf of jalapeño-cheddar bread and two PBRs. Co-owners Eric Barton & Tom Marrin have a few things in common but the strongest bond is their love of a good deli sandwich.

name: Golden Rotisserie
self_description: We're a family-owned and operated rotisserie restaurant nestled in the mountain town of Truckee, California. What started as a shared dream around our dinner table turned into a community staple built on tradition, hard work, and the kind of meals that feel like home.

name: Gravity Haus Truckee-Tahoe
self_description: Gravity Haus was built on a simple belief: life is better when movement, community, and meaningful places are woven into the everyday rhythm.

name: High Altitude Fitness
self_description: High Altitude Fitness has been serving the greater Lake Tahoe community for over 15 years. We take pride in helping our members live life to the fullest. Our mission is to provide a facility and an empowered staff that helps individuals and the community on their journey to health and wellness.

name: Moi's Thai Cafe
self_description: Nestled in the heart of Penn Valley, Moi's Thai Café is a delightful culinary gem that brings the vibrant flavors of Thailand straight to 11332 Pleasant Valley Rd.

name: Montoliva Vineyard & Winery
self_description: Founded in 2000, Montoliva Vineyard & Winery creates Tuscan-inspired wines of uncommon depth and character.

name: Moody's Bistro Bar and Beats
self_description: Established in 2002, Moody's Bistro is the quintessential Truckee experience.

name: Mountain Lotus Cafe
self_description: Mountain Lotus is more than a yoga studio. It is a place to move, breathe, nourish, and connect.

name: Old Town Tap
self_description: We set out to bring something different and amazing to the Truckee community. Our passion is creating delicious food, an amazing bar program and top notch service that's local, seasonal and sustainable.

name: PJ's at Grays Crossing
self_description: PJ's at Gray's Crossing is the ultimate outdoor dining destination in Truckee, California.

name: Pianeta
self_description: We are a cozy restaurant located in the heart of historic downtown Truckee, specializing in creating delicious Italian foods, homemade pastas, and stellar dining service since 1998.

name: Sierra Bakehouse
self_description: Sierra Bakehouse was created by two friends, Daniella and Kristy, with a love and passion for baking.

name: Smokey's Kitchen
self_description: Smokey's Kitchen is a family-friendly breakfast, lunch and dinner restaurant, with fast and friendly service in Truckee, California.

name: Squeeze In
self_description: Nestled in the Sierra Nevada mountain town of Truckee, CA, Squeeze In first opened its doors in 1974. At just 10 feet wide and 62 feet long, guests literally have to 'squeeze in' to enjoy the cozy breakfast nook that offers 'the best omelettes on the planet' alongside boozy mimosas, eclectic decor, and unforgettable charm.

name: Stella
self_description: Soulful, Locally Sourced California Cuisine. Fresh, Vibrant, and Innovative.

name: Tacos Jalisco
self_description: Tacos Jalisco is your destination for authentic Mexican street tacos, family-owned and operated, with deep roots in the bold, vibrant flavors of Jalisco, Mexico.

name: Tangerine French Bistro
self_description: Nestled in Truckee's vibrant downtown, Tangerine is a classic French bistro, brought to life by the creators of Great Gold.

name: Thai Delicacy
self_description: Only the Freshest Ingredients Served

name: The Pub at Donner Lake
self_description: Fresh food. Friendly Service. Beautiful Views. We're your neighborhood home

name: The Richardson House
self_description: We are proud of The Richardson House and relish in its history and beauty. Our mission is to create an experience worthy of the house's beautiful surroundings in what will be an incredible getaway.

name: The Truckee Hotel
self_description: Established in 1873, the Truckee Hotel is a staple in tales of Truckee's past. Touched by many as a stagecoach stop on the Dutch Flat Wagon Road, the Truckee Hotel remains a drop-in for Sierra visitors, historians, adventure seekers, foodies, actors, and musicians.

name: Truckee Donner Lodge
self_description: Located in the town of Truckee, CA amidst mountains, lakes, alpines, and meadows, the Truckee Donner Lodge is a warm relaxing space so you can rest and recharge. Our lodge combines modern luxuries and contemporary decor with the traditional amenities and architecture of a country lodge.

name: Truckee River Winery
self_description: Since 1989, the Jones family has been dedicated to the artistry of high-altitude viticulture. What began as Truckee River Winery has grown into Truckee Family Wines, now encompassing both our original homestead in Truckee and our new 174 Winery in Cedar Ridge, CA.

name: Tuscany Gardens Pizzeria
self_description: You don't have to go far to get an authentic taste of Italy. Our family has owned and operated our two stores for over 30 years!

name: Twelve 28 Kitchen
self_description: A warm & comfortable social gathering place where our passion is on food that we make from the heart and the people we get to enjoy it with!

name: West River House
self_description: West River House is a historic gem nestled in the heart of downtown Truckee, North Lake Tahoe. Whether you are just passing through on the train or here for a ski, mountain bike or family vacation, we keep things simple and efficient to provide you with the best value.

name: Zano's Family Italian & Pizzeria
self_description: Our restaurant combines the flavor of Italy with the hospitality of the Sierra Nevada. This is a warm and friendly environment and we would like to keep it that way. At Zano's, we feel that it is simply a genuine belief in ourselves that sets us apart from the competition.

name: Zumo Wine
self_description: ZUMO WINE IS A SMALL WINERY BASED IN NORTHERN CALIFORNIA. IT WAS STARTED IN 2018. ALL OF OUR WINES ARE MADE WITH ORGANIC GRAPES.

name: Donner Memorial State Park Museum
self_description: Donner Memorial State Park offers hiking and recreation opportunities along Donner Lake and contains a historic monument and visitor center exhibits paying tribute to pioneers crossing the Sierra Nevada.

name: Gallery 5830'
self_description: Gallery 5830' is a family- and artist-owned fine art gallery in Downtown Truckee featuring original work by local and regional artists

name: La Galleria
self_description: La Galleria has been a staple in the local community for over 38 years. We provide treasures from around the world featuring one of a kind gifts for any occasion, any age group, of all prices.

name: Made By Me in Truckee
self_description: Step into Made By Me, a delightful creative haven in downtown Truckee, California, founded by Suzette Birnbaum, who earned her degree in education and art from Santa Clara University. With a rich background in art and education, Suzette invites you to explore your artistic side in a space designed for creativity and connection.

name: Piper J Gallery
self_description: Piper J Gallery is a welcoming art venue located in Truckee, CA showcasing the work by a wide variety of art and artists. Since it's establishment in 2016, Piper J Gallery has been a place where collectors can discover contemporary art for modern mountain living.

name: Riverside Studios
self_description: Riverside Studios is a unique art gallery and gift shop in downtown Truckee. It is owned and operated by four woman artists: Mary Guerra, Alanna Hughes, Kahlil Johnson and Sondrea Larsen.

name: Steve Randall Community Recreation Center
self_description: The Steve Randall Community Recreation Center is a multi-use facility complete with a gymnasium, fitness center, climbing wall, dance room, meeting rooms, kitchen, and more.
notes: found on home page; descriptive but not first-person narrative

name: Truckee Dance Factory
self_description: Truckee Dance Factory (TDF) is the premier dance studio serving the North Lake Tahoe area. Offering a variety of programs for students ages 10 months and up, TDF inspires a love of dance in a supportive space that fosters creativity, self-confidence, and self-discipline.

name: Truckee Railroad Museum
self_description: The Truckee Railroad Museum is located in a former Southern Pacific Railroad bay-window caboose next to the historic railroad depot in downtown Truckee.
notes: descriptive but not first-person; venue does not provide mission or 'we are' statement

name: Bluestone Jewelry & Wine
self_description: Opened in 2007 in Tahoe city Linda's favorite jewelry store was closing. As many people, they came originally for skiing Marc and Linda decided to take a chance and purchased the storefront.

name: Buoy & Trap Seafood Market
self_description: Owned and operated by a native Rhode Island seafood connoisseur with years of experience in the food and beverage industry, we are bringing a unique and bespoke product to the Truckee Tahoe region.

name: Cabona's
self_description: Cabona's has been part of Truckee's story since 1906, when Dave Cabona opened a Dry Goods Emporium stocked with everything mountain life required. As the town evolved, so did we, trading in rubber and reels for flannel and fashion, bringing a touch of style to Sierra living.

name: California 89
self_description: California 89 was born from a love of adventure and a deep connection to the Sierra. Rooted in the Wild. Built by Family.

name: Cultural Threads
self_description: Connecting you with artisans at 14,000 ft. in the Peruvian Andes. Cultural Threads empowers indigenous women to preserve natural alpaca fibers and handspun traditions by creating sustainable knits that are good for humanity and the environment.

name: Echo Blue
self_description: Locally owned and operated in Truckee, CA, Echo Blue introduces mountain chic to up-cycled women's fashion.

name: Hello Clogs
self_description: Hello Clogs is a small-batch line of handmade wooden clogs crafted in the foothills of Nevada City, California by designer and maker Carabeth Rowley. Hello Clogs celebrates traditional techniques, modern design, and everyday wearability.

name: The Farmacy
self_description: A non-profit food hub operates as a vital link between local farmers and the community, ensuring that fresh, sustainably grown food reaches the people who need it most.
notes: Descriptive mission statement, not first-person narrative

name: The Sock Parlour
self_description: We love socks and everything cozy. We are so much more than a sock store, we offer dozens of USA made/small batch makers.

name: White Buffalo
self_description: We sell mountain, southwestern and regional jewelry, art, gifts and home decor handcrafted by independent artisans.

name: Wildflower Nursery
self_description: At Wildflower we believe that nurseries should be magical places. We are a full service nursery located in Penn Valley, California, with a vast assortment of both indoor and outdoor plants and gifts.

name: Community Arts Center
self_description: TDRPD has been providing recreation and park services since 1963. The District philosophy is to provide recreation and park programs and facilities for everyone in the community.

name: Adele's Attic
self_description: Adele's Attic specializes in both hand-selected luxury vintage and pre-loved designer clothing and accessories. This is a unique shopping experience for those who value wearable art through fashion while highlighting sustainability.

name: Valhalla Vintage
self_description: Valhalla was established in 2017 followed by our Brick & Mortar vintage shop which came to Truckee, California in 2022. Our shop features Americana heritage brands, timeless classics and one of a kind pieces from years of collecting.

name: Beaver Pond Route Trail
self_description: Our mission is to awaken the adventurer inside everyone. Whether it's your first step into the uncharted, or your annual backcountry trip, onX helps bring that adventure to life.
notes: Self-description is from onX (trail database company), not the trail venue itself

name: Billy Hills OHV Trail
self_description: Billy Hill OHV Route is 1.53 miles long and open to all high clearance/4WD vehicles. This is a loop with several off-shoot roads.
notes: Functional description from government agency, not self-description by the venue

name: Black Swan Trail
self_description: Built by BYLT's trail team and volunteers, the community-supported trail weaves through blue oak and gray pine woodlands.
notes: BYLT (Bear Yuba Land Trust) is the trail manager; found descriptive passage about trail construction/community involvement

name: Donner Summit Canyon
self_description: Purchased by the Land Trust in 2010 and conveyed to California State Parks in 2012 as an addition to Donner Memorial State Park, Donner Summit Canyon is the gateway to perhaps the most important square mile in California's history.
notes: Clear first-person organizational voice ('purchased by the Land Trust'); meaningful self-description with historical significance

name: Park and Walk Trail: Chicago Park
self_description: This trail was built by the community so parents could drive their kids to the village center, park, and then walk them out to the elementary school.
notes: Clear origin story and community purpose; meaningful self-description explaining the trail's reason for existing

name: Rambler Trail: Clover Valley Preserve
self_description: In December 2014, the Bach family – owners of the property since the 1970s – generously donated the land to BYLT for the purpose of protecting the land from development forever.
notes: Clear preservation story with community donation; meaningful self-description from BYLT about the preserve's origin

name: Shoreline Park
self_description: TDRPD has been providing recreation and park services since 1963.
notes: Found on about page for Truckee-Donner Recreation & Park District; institutional mission statement

name: Truckee River Legacy Trail
self_description: The Truckee River Legacy Trail follows its namesake river's south side from downtown Truckee to the Glenshire neighborhood.
notes: TrailLink description; factual but not first-person from the venue itself

name: Yuba Blue
self_description: Yuba Blue was founded in 1995 by Sarah Lazard. From the day she opened the doors, Yuba Blue was a local favorite and a must-see for tourists visiting the area. Yuba Blue remains a small business run by generations of creative women who are active in day-to-day operations.
notes: Verbatim from About Yuba Blue section

name: 1882 Bar & Grill
self_description: The newest addition to the river Street Inn is 1882, a restaurant named after the year the building was built. Offering a full bar and BBQ style menu, meats are smoked daily on our authentic Texas smoker. Also, we are the only restaurant serving delicious Beignets daily.
notes: Verbatim from 1882 Bar & Grill section of River Street Inn site

name: Gray Pine Winery
self_description: Named for the native California Gray Pine which is prevalent in our area, Gray Pine Vineyard & Winery is a small, limited-production winery located in Penn Valley, California, in the Sierra Foothills, with an annual production of approximately 300 cases. We grow our own grapes on our two-acre vineyard and make our own wines. Specializing in Bordeaux red varietals, we produce Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Cabernet Franc, Petit Verdot, Malbec and a classic Bordeaux-style Red Blend called PennRidge.
notes: Verbatim mission/intro from homepage

name: Jax At The Tracks
self_description: At Jax At The Tracks, we’re all about comfort food with a twist. Located at 10144 W River St, Truckee, CA, we bring you the flavors of classic American diner fare in a welcoming, casual atmosphere.
notes: Verbatim from Welcome to JAX section

name: River Street Inn
self_description: The River Street Inn offers small Hotel charm in historic Old Town Truckee. A perfect vacation getaway, just minutes from Lake Tahoe. Built in 1882, this Bed and Breakfast sits on the Truckee River and located in the heart of Old Town.
notes: Verbatim from homepage welcome section

name: Village Pizzaria
self_description: Since 1981 the Village Pizzeria has been family owned. It originally opened in Tahoe City and then relocated to its current location in Truckee, California. The Village Pizzeria has been a local favorite and is proud to be a part of the Truckee Tahoe community.
notes: Verbatim from homepage intro

name: Nomad Boutique
self_description: Nomad Boutique is your go-to destination for the latest in women’s fashion, with two locations in Midtown Reno, Nevada and Downtown Truckee, California. Founded by Allison Schultz, a Truckee local with a love for style and community, Nomad has grown over the past 10 years into one of the best boutiques in Reno and Truckee—trusted by locals and adored by visitors.
notes: Verbatim from About Us page

name: Donner Memorial State Park
self_description: Offers hiking and recreation opportunities along Donner Lake and contains a historic monument and visitor center exhibits paying tribute to pioneers crossing the Sierra Nevada.
notes: Verbatim from official CA State Parks 'About Donner Memorial State Park' section; steward's own description
